summary: In a Q&A reflecting on his 30-year career at Harvard, epidemiologist Meir Stampfer discusses his initial draw to the field for its focus on disease prevention. He highlights the integration of biomarkers as epidemiology's greatest recent advancement and advises students to prioritize impactful, novel research over mere career advancement.

principles.statement: Prioritize impactful research over career advancement.
principles.rationale: Career advancement is necessary, but it is merely a tool to enable you to work on important projects that have a real impact, rather than being the ultimate goal itself.
principles.source_quote: Of course one needs to have career advancement, but many seem to forget that the career is a means to an end, not the end itself.

principles.statement: The core goal of epidemiological research is prevention.
principles.rationale: The ultimate purpose of studying the etiology of chronic diseases is to find actionable ways to keep people healthy.
principles.source_quote: My research goals have remained the same for all these years, which is expand the scope for prevention and figure out ways to stay healthy.

heuristics: Keep a piece of paper on your desk to write down your bigger mistakes, with the hope of avoiding them in the future.

signature_quotes.text: My research goals have remained the same for all these years, which is expand the scope for prevention and figure out ways to stay healthy.
signature_quotes.context: When asked about what drew him to epidemiology and his long-term goals.

signature_quotes.text: I kept a piece of paper on my desk where I wrote down my bigger mistakes, with the hope of avoiding them in the future – but there are lots of ways to mess up!
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on his time as chair of the Department of Epidemiology.

signature_quotes.text: I think the greatest advance has been integration of biomarkers, including all the ‘omics, into epidemiology research.
signature_quotes.context: When asked about the most exciting recent developments in the field of epidemiology.

signature_quotes.text: Of course one needs to have career advancement, but many seem to forget that the career is a means to an end, not the end itself.
signature_quotes.context: Giving advice to current epidemiology students.

signature_quotes.text: The overall quality of epidemiology papers these days is higher than in the past, but still many papers are boring and do not address novel or pressing issues.
signature_quotes.context: Critiquing the current state of academic publishing in epidemiology.

anti_patterns.description: Treating career advancement as the ultimate goal rather than a tool.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distracts researchers from putting their energy into the most important projects where they can have a genuine impact.

anti_patterns.description: Publishing research papers that do not address novel or pressing issues.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Even if the methodological quality of the paper is high, the research ends up being boring and fails to advance the field meaningfully.
